regimeSub: Warsh signals July rate decision pending while AI-driven equity melt-up defies 4.2% CPI and bond selloff.
themes: Warsh at Sintra vowed to 'disappoint' inflation doves, hinted July FOMC decision → USD bid, DXY 101.3, gold -worst month since 2008
BOJ hiked to 1% on Jun-16, ending tapering FY27 → USDJPY blows through 162.5, yen carry trade accelerating into risk assets
KOSPI +68% Q2 best since 1998 as SK Hynix overtakes Samsung in market cap → HBM/AI capex cycle overwhelms KRW weakness at 1553
US CPI 4.2% YoY in May print sparked fastest inflation since 2023 → 10Y at 4.44% as Fed removes cutting bias, fiscal concerns mount

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Real rate ~0% (4.44% 10Y minus 4.2% CPI); Warsh hawkish tilt could push positive in H2

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: Curve +30bp steepening (2Y 4.14%, 10Y 4.44%); bear steepener on fiscal/inflation premium

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads compressed despite rate vol; AI euphoria and resilient labor (172K May NFP) supporting risk

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: VIX 16.6 complacent given 4.2% CPI and Warsh hike signals; positioning for summer grind higher

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $70.9 collapsed from $120 Iran peak; ceasefire talks and oversupply narrative dominating

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: Gold $4061 suffering worst monthly drop since 2008; USD strength and real rate fears crushing longs

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: KRW at 1553 breaching key level; foreign outflows $941M as 'Sell Korea' rout tests BOK resolve

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: SOX ripping on Nvidia +85% Q1 rev, TSMC capex raise; SK Hynix > Samsung in market cap on HBM demand

headline: New Fed Chair Warsh holds rates but warns of July hike as 4.2% CPI and AI-driven equity mania collide with BOJ normalization and yen collapse to 162.5.
